title: How to Choose the Right Bathroom Tile
author: Calvin Berry
excerpt: Tile can make or break a bathroom remodel. Here's what 50 years of experience has taught us about choosing tile that looks great and lasts.
content:
## Why Tile Choice Matters

Tile is the most visible element in most bathrooms. It sets the mood, affects maintenance, and needs to hold up to moisture, heat, and daily use for decades. Choosing the wrong tile is an expensive mistake — choosing the right one makes your bathroom a pleasure to use every day.

## Floor Tile: Safety Comes First

For bathroom floors, slip resistance is non-negotiable. Look for tiles with a textured or matte finish rather than polished surfaces. Good floor tile options include:

- **Porcelain**: The workhorse of bathroom flooring — durable, water-resistant, and available in countless styles
- **Natural stone with honed finish**: Marble, slate, or travertine add luxury but require sealing
- **Small-format mosaic**: The extra grout lines actually improve grip

Avoid large-format polished tiles on bathroom floors — they look great in showrooms but become dangerously slick when wet.

## Shower Tile: Think Long-Term

Shower walls need to handle constant water exposure. Porcelain and ceramic are reliable choices. Natural stone is beautiful but requires annual sealing and more maintenance.

For shower floors specifically, small mosaic tiles (2x2 inch or smaller) are ideal because they conform to the slope toward the drain and provide excellent traction.

## Popular Tile Styles Right Now

- **Subway tile**: A classic that never goes out of style — 3x6 is standard, but 4x12 elongated subway is trending
- **Large format**: 12x24 or bigger creates a clean, modern look with fewer grout lines
- **Patterned cement tile**: Bold and beautiful, but best used as an accent
- **Wood-look porcelain**: Warm aesthetic with waterproof durability

## Grout Matters More Than You Think

Don't overlook grout selection. Epoxy grout costs more upfront but resists stains and mildew far better than standard cement grout. It's especially worth it in showers and on floors.

Color-wise, a grout close to your tile color creates a seamless look. Contrasting grout (like dark grout with white subway tile) is a bold choice that shows every imperfection in layout — make sure your installer is experienced.

## Our Recommendation

For most bathroom remodels, we recommend porcelain tile for both floors and walls. It offers the best combination of durability, water resistance, style options, and value. Pair it with epoxy grout and you'll have a bathroom that looks great for 20+ years with minimal maintenance.

Ready to start your bathroom remodel? [Get a free estimate](/quote.html) and we'll help you choose the perfect tile for your space.
